data.name: Description
data.value: Diagnose Aspire environment issues and verify setup

data.name: JsonOptionDescription
data.value: Output format (Table or Json)

data.name: EnvironmentCheckHeader
data.value: Aspire Environment Check

data.name: AspireCategoryHeader
data.value: Aspire

data.name: AppHostCategoryHeader
data.value: AppHost

data.name: SdkCategoryHeader
data.value: .NET SDK

data.name: ContainerCategoryHeader
data.value: Container Runtime

data.name: EnvironmentCategoryHeader
data.value: Environment

data.name: SummaryFormat
data.value: Summary: {0} passed, {1} warnings, {2} failed

data.name: DetailedPrerequisitesLink
data.value: For detailed prerequisites: {0}
data.comment: {0} is a clickable URL

data.name: CheckingPrerequisites
data.value: Checking Aspire environment...

data.name: OperatingSystemMessageFormat
data.value: Operating system: {0}
data.comment: {0} is the operating system display name and version, for example "Windows 10.0.22631", "macOS 26.0", or "Linux Ubuntu 24.04".

data.name: VersionUnknown
data.value: unknown

data.name: CliVersionMessageFormat
data.value: Aspire CLI version {0}
data.comment: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version.

data.name: CliVersionOutOfDateMessageFormat
data.value: Aspire CLI version {0} is out of date. Latest version is {1}
data.comment: {0} is the current Aspire CLI version. {1} is the latest available Aspire CLI version.

data.name: CliVersionOutOfDateFixFormat
data.value: Run '{0}' to update Aspire CLI.
data.comment: {0} is the command to update Aspire CLI.

data.name: CliVersionUpdateCheckFailedMessage
data.value: Could not check for Aspire CLI updates

data.name: ChannelSuffixFormat
data.value: (channel: {0})
data.comment: {0} is the Aspire channel name (e.g., "stable", "staging", "daily", "local", "pr-1234"). Appended to CLI / AppHost version messages.

data.name: AppHostVersionMessageFormat
data.value: AppHost version {0} ({1})
data.comment: {0} is the AppHost Aspire.Hosting version. {1} is the AppHost project path.

data.name: AppHostVersionUnknownMessageFormat
data.value: Could not determine AppHost version ({0})
data.comment: {0} is the AppHost project path.

data.name: AppHostVersionCheckFailedMessage
data.value: Could not determine AppHost version

data.name: DevCertsNoCertificateMessage
data.value: No HTTPS development certificate found

data.name: DevCertsNoCertificateDetails
data.value: Aspire uses HTTPS for secure communication between the dashboard and your services during local development.

data.name: DevCertsTrustFixFormat
data.value: Run '{0}' to trust the HTTPS development certificate.
data.comment: {0} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

data.name: DevCertsCleanAndTrustFixFormat
data.value: Run '{0}' to remove all certificates, then run '{1}' to create and trust a new one.
data.comment: {0} is the aspire certs clean command, {1} is the aspire certs trust command (not localizable)

data.name: DevCertsMultipleNoneTrustedMessageFormat
data.value: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates), but none are trusted

data.name: DevCertsMultipleNoneTrustedDetailsFormat
data.value: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion.

data.name: DevCertsMultipleSomeUntrustedMessageFormat
data.value: Multiple HTTPS development certificates found ({0} certificates)

data.name: DevCertsMultipleSomeUntrustedDetailsFormat
data.value: Found certificates: {0}. Having multiple certificates can cause confusion when selecting which one to use.

data.name: DevCertsTrustedMessage
data.value: HTTPS development certificate is trusted

data.name: DevCertsNotTrustedMessage
data.value: HTTPS development certificate is not trusted

data.name: DevCertsNotTrustedDetailsFormat
data.value: Certificate {0} exists in the personal store but was not found in the trusted root store.

data.name: DevCertsPartiallyTrustedMessage
data.value: HTTPS development certificate is only partially trusted

data.name: DevCertsPartiallyTrustedDetailsFormat
data.value: The certificate is in the trusted store, but SSL_CERT_DIR is not configured to include '{0}'. Some applications may not trust the certificate. 'aspire run' will configure this automatically.

data.name: DevCertsPartiallyTrustedFixFormat
data.value: Set SSL_CERT_DIR in your shell profile: {0}

data.name: DevCertsOldVersionMessageFormat
data.value: HTTPS development certificate has an older version ({0})

data.name: DevCertsOldVersionDetailsFormat
data.value: Older certificate versions (< v{0}) may not support all certificate trust scenarios.

data.name: DcpBundleNotFoundMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) bundle not found; skipping connection health checks

data.name: DcpBundleNotFoundDetails
data.value: The running Aspire CLI is not associated with a bundle layout that contains the Developer Control Plane (DCP).

data.name: DcpExecutableNotFoundMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable not found

data.name: DcpExecutableNotFoundDetailsFormat
data.value: Expected Developer Control Plane (DCP) executable at '{0}'.
data.comment: {0} is the expected DCP executable path.

data.name: DcpConnectionCheckFailedMessage
data.value: Failed to check Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health

data.name: DcpApiServerReturnedHttpStatusMessageFormat
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) API server returned HTTP {0} ({1}).
data.comment: {0} is the numeric HTTP status code. {1} is the HTTP status code name.

data.name: DcpEphemeralCertificateConnectionSucceededMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate succeeded

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateConnectionSucceededMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate succeeded

data.name: DcpConnectionSucceededMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health checks succeeded

data.name: DcpConnectionTimedOutMessageFormat
data.value: Timed out after {0} seconds while checking Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection health.
data.comment: {0} is the timeout in seconds.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateUnavailableMessage
data.value: No trusted developer certificate was available for the Developer Control Plane (DCP) developer certificate connection check

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateTrustFix
data.value: Run `aspire certs trust` to create and trust a developer certificate.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateRepairTrustFix
data.value: Run `aspire certs trust` to repair developer certificate trust.

data.name: DcpTlsAuthenticationFailedDetailsFormat
data.value: TLS authentication failed: {0}
data.comment: {0} is the TLS authentication error message.

data.name: DcpEphemeralCertificateConnectionFailedMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using an ephemeral DCP-managed certificate failed

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateConnectionFailedMessage
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) connection using the developer certificate failed

data.name: DcpStartFailedMessage
data.value: Failed to start Developer Control Plane (DCP).

data.name: DcpExitedBeforeKubeconfigDetailsFormat
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) exited before writing kubeconfig. Exit code: {0}.{1}{2}
data.comment: {0} is the DCP process exit code. {1} is a newline. {2} is recent DCP process output.

data.name: DcpNoOutputDetails
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) did not write any output.

data.name: DcpKubeconfigMissingServerDetails
data.value: Developer Control Plane (DCP) kubeconfig did not contain a valid server URI.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateInvalidForCacheDetails
data.value: The developer certificate could not be cached because it was not valid.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateUserProfileMissingDetails
data.value: The developer certificate could not be cached because the user profile directory could not be determined.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateNoTrustedExportableDetails
data.value: No fully trusted exportable developer certificate with a private key was found.

data.name: DcpDeveloperCertificateMissingThumbprintDetails
data.value: The developer certificate did not have a thumbprint.

data.name: DevCertsTrustLabelFull
data.value: [trusted]

data.name: DevCertsTrustLabelPartial
data.value: [partial]

data.name: HeaderInstallations
data.value: Aspire CLI Installations

data.name: ColumnPath
data.value: Path

data.name: ColumnVersion
data.value: Version

data.name: ColumnChannel
data.value: Channel

data.name: ColumnRoute
data.value: Route

data.name: ColumnPathStatus
data.value: PATH status

data.name: ValueCurrentMarker
data.value: (current)
data.comment: Suffix appended to the running CLI's path so the user can identify which install is themselves.

data.name: ValueUnknown
data.value: (unknown)
data.comment: Placeholder shown in the table when a field could not be determined for a discovered install.

data.name: ValueNotProbed
data.value: (not probed)
data.comment: Placeholder shown in the table when an install was found but not asked to self-describe (e.g., trust gate).

data.name: ValueProbeFailed
data.value: (probe failed)
data.comment: Placeholder shown in the table when an install was probed but did not return usable data.

data.name: ValuePathActive
data.value: active
data.comment: Shown when an install is the first Aspire CLI resolved from PATH.

data.name: ValuePathShadowed
data.value: shadowed
data.comment: Shown when an install is on PATH but an earlier Aspire CLI entry shadows it.

data.name: ValuePathNotOnPath
data.value: not on PATH
data.comment: Shown when an install was not discovered from PATH.

data.name: InstallationDiscoveryFailedReason
data.value: Install discovery failed. See the Aspire CLI logs for details.

data.name: LegacySettingsDetectedMessageFormat
data.value: Legacy .aspire/settings.json detected at {0}
data.comment: {0} is the full path to the legacy settings file.

data.name: LegacySettingsDetectedFix
data.value: Run 'aspire init' (or any aspire run/add/update/pipeline command) to migrate to aspire.config.json. The legacy file continues to work — this is a non-blocking warning.
